steps.title: Clarify what “historical similarity” means
steps.content: The phrase “historical similarity” asks us to show that The Peak and Gehry House played parallel roles in the development of deconstructivist architecture. The notes identify the 1988 MoMA exhibition as the moment this design movement became influential—the defining historical context for both projects.

steps.title: Match both projects to their shared historical moment
steps.content: D presents both The Peak and Gehry House as featured in the 1988 exhibition that popularized deconstructivist design, establishing them as equivalent anchors in a defining historical moment. C emphasizes shared design traits (jagged geometric forms and subverting conventional assumptions) rather than historical parallel; A and B fail to draw clear equivalence between the projects. That matches choice D — D is correct.
